title: Bahrain F1 Pre-Season Testing Explained — What We Actually Learned
description:
Bahrain pre-season testing is over — but the lap times didn’t tell the real story.

Behind the calm headlines and misleading timesheets, teams revealed far more than they wanted to. Confidence issues, hidden pace, early struggles, and subtle signs of who understands the new regulations… and who doesn’t.

In this video, we break down what actually mattered during Bahrain testing:
• Which teams looked comfortable
• Who might be hiding performance

title: Barcelona Shakedown EXPLAINED: Every F1 2026 Team’s Real Situation
description:
F1 2026 finally hit the track for real in Barcelona – and the first five days of running have given us a very early look at the new era.

On paper, Lewis Hamilton was fastest of the week for Ferrari with a 1:16.348.

But when you dig into the data, it’s Mercedes – with 500 laps on the W17 – and the power unit manufacturers who really stand out.

In this video, we go team by team through the full Barcelona shakedown:

• What each team actually did on track

title: Ferrari 2026 Engine Fire-Up Explained | Lewis Hamilton, New Rules & F1’s Electric Future
description:
The first fire-up of Ferrari’s 2026 Formula 1 engine wasn’t just a technical milestone — it was a message to the entire grid. After months of doubts, delays, and speculation, this moment confirms Ferrari is no longer reacting to the new era. They are shaping it.

title: F1, Ferrari SF-26 in-depth: suspension, engine, and aerodynamic choices explained
description: The competitive success of the Ferrari SF-26 remains to be discovered, and only the race track will ultimately provide definitive answers. However, regardless of how competitive the new car will eventually prove to be over the course of the season, the latest Maranello project already stands out for highlighting a noticeably more courageous and less conservative technical approach.
